required_inputs: request intent and requested acute care operational action
acute care operations boundary, decision-rights matrix, and adjacent specialist map
clinical, payer, privacy, coding, revenue-cycle, legal, financial approval, and patient-safety authority indicators

request asks for payer-policy interpretation, code assignment, claim approval, PHI disclosure approval, write-off, appeal strategy, or financial override

required_inputs: public healthcare regulator, payer/provider, workflow, and terminology sources
tenant policy, source hierarchy, payer and plan context, and source freshness metadata
current facility, service-line, care-setting, and jurisdiction context

public guidance, tenant policy, payer rule, clinical instruction, facility procedure, contract term, or jurisdictional requirement materially conflicts

required_inputs: EHR encounter, ADT status, order, clinical-administrative note, case management note, utilization review record, discharge or transition record, payer portal evidence, charge, coding, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, and audit log
patient/member status, service date, payer, plan, authorization, referral, eligibility, privacy, and source-of-truth hierarchy
completion, exception, and audit evidence requirements
refusal_triggers: critical acute care artifact is absent, stale, contradictory, not retrievable, or not authoritative
traceability from blocker to source record to accountable owner to completion evidence cannot be established

required_inputs: systems-of-record map across EHR, EMR, ADT, case management, utilization review, patient access, payer portal, practice management, revenue cycle, coding, claims, document repository, privacy, and audit systems
tenant RACI, queue routing policy, source hierarchy, escalation SLA, and prerequisite rules
authorization, referral, eligibility, order, documentation, discharge, transition, coding, claim, denial, appeal, privacy, and access dependencies
refusal_triggers: critical dependency owner is unresolved

authority completeness signals
policy and source conflict indicators
clinical, patient-safety, privacy, payer, coding, revenue-cycle, legal, financial, and access-risk indicators

clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, coding interpretation, revenue-cycle authority, patient-safety issue, owner conflict, or source conflict prevents safe synthesis

required_inputs: adjacent specialist map
authority boundary and handoff prerequisites
evidence package required by patient access, utilization management, clinical review, coding, revenue cycle, privacy, compliance, or payer operations

required_inputs: accepted acute care operational request
tenant lifecycle-state definitions and closure criteria
current source artifacts, owner, due date, escalation SLA, and system links
refusal_triggers: state cannot move forward because prerequisite evidence, source record, owner acceptance, system access, or approval is missing

required_inputs: exception type, severity, operational impact, patient/member context, owner, due time, escalation rule, and source evidence

tenant prioritization, rework, escalation, and closure rules

operational impact cannot be assessed without missing records or owner confirmation
policy conflict, source conflict, patient-safety indicator, or privacy risk prevents administrative resolution

owner acceptance, downstream handoff, residual blocker, audit log, and system-of-record links
reopen criteria and source-freshness confirmation

required_inputs: request content and patient-safety indicators
clinical protocol, diagnosis, treatment, discharge readiness, level-of-care, medical-necessity, and licensed-review authority map
administrative facts that can be summarized safely

operational delay includes patient-safety indicators that require licensed review

required_inputs: PHI handling context, role-based access posture, consent/proxy and minimum-necessary rules
